content:
# Sabkha Sedimentology

**Summary**: The study of the sedimentary processes and characteristics of sabkhas.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-armoh|Armoh, 2016]] focused on sabkha-sedimentology to characterize the environment of Sabkha Jayb Uwayyid.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] analyzed sabkha-sedimentology to characterize the distribution of gypsum, anhydrite, and halite.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2016-armoh|Armoh, 2016]] ['evaporation rate', 'soil texture', 'sedimentology']

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] analyzed sabkha-sedimentology to characterize the distribution of gypsum, anhydrite, and halite.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2016-armoh|Armoh, 2016]] ['portable-chamber', 'sieve-analysis']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Sabkha Jayb Uwayyid is composed of three main layers: a sandy soil layer (15m), a second layer (113m), and a third layer. ([[2016-armoh|Armoh, 2016, p. 23]])

- Sedimentary facies are characterized by a central halite crust at the margin of water ponds surrounded by gypsum mush. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 12]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2016-armoh|Armoh, 2016]] Quantifying the impact of salinity and grain size on evaporation rates.
